intent: Cross-version bounds check. The bounds_test module (from _policies/bounds_test.te) is compiled with checkmodule on el8 (policyvers 31) and installed via semodule -n into a bind-mounted tmpfs copy of the el8 module store. The rsbnd_child_t -> tmp_t:file { write lock ioctl } denial is replayed against the resulting policy.bounds.el8. El8 result: 'Typebounds violation' (Bounds bucket) - matches the el10 result from rocky10-typebounds-multi. Confirms: the Bounds bucket is stable across policyvers 31/33/36.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category=Bounds (libsepol audit2why reports 'Typebounds violation. Add an allow rule for the parent type.'). floor_label=none (Bounds is NOT detectable from the AVC record alone - no role/level/permissive signal). This is the headline cross-version proof that Bounds is stable.

oracle.audit2why_detail: Typebounds violation. Add an allow rule for the parent type. (repeated for each of the 3 denials: write, lock, ioctl)
oracle.build_note: checkmodule -M -m succeeded on el8 (policyvers ceiling 31, module language 4-19). The bounds_test module uses base-module syntax (module ... 1.0; require { ... }) which is backward-compatible to el8's module language level.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Typebounds violation: rsbnd_child_t is bounded by rsbnd_parent_t but rsbnd_child_t has { write lock ioctl } permissions on tmp_t:file that rsbnd_parent_t does not. A TE allow for rsbnd_child_t cannot fix a typebounds violation; the fix is to add the same permissions to the PARENT type or remove the typebounds relationship.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl emits 'allow rsbnd_child_t tmp_t:file { write lock ioctl }' - which is exactly what audit2allow -N suggests. But this TE allow already exists in the policy (it was authored in bounds_test.te). The typebounds constraint overrides it. Only the authoritative layer catches Bounds; the floor heuristic sees no role/level/permissive signal and would suggest the allow.

xver_baseline.policyvers_note: The typebounds mechanism is enforced by the kernel and replayed by libsepol identically across policyvers 31 (el8), 33 (el9/el10), and 36 (el10). The 'Typebounds violation' message in audit2why.txt is word-for-word identical across all three. Bounds bucket is stable.
xver_baseline.checkmodule_note: checkmodule ceiling for el8 is policyvers 19 (checkpolicy 2.9). The bounds_test module uses base-module syntax and compiled cleanly. El10 uses checkmodule 4-24 (policyvers ceiling 36). The compiled module binary differs (different format version) but the behavior is identical.
